prompt: How many pages if the 2023 IPCC report (85 pages version) mentions nuclear energy?
actual:
## Nuclear Energy in the IPCC AR6 Synthesis Report: Climate Change 2023

I downloaded and analyzed all three versions of the IPCC AR6 Synthesis Report directly from the official IPCC website (ipcc.ch):

| Version | Pages | Pages mentioning "nuclear" | Nuclear page numbers |
|---------|-------|----------------------------|----------------------|
| Summary for Policymakers (SPM) | 42 | **1** | Page 33 |
| Longer Report (Sections) | 81 | **1** | Page 69 |
| Full Volume (SPM + Sections + Annexes) | 186 | **3** | Pages 43, 119, 171 |

### Answer for the "85 pages version"
The **Longer Report (Sections)** at **81 pages** is the closest match to the "85 pages version" you refer to. In this version, nuclear energy is mentioned on **1 page** (page 69).

### Context of the mentions
In all versions, "Nuclear" appears in the same context: as one item in a **figure/diagram listing energy supply mitigation options**, alongside Solar, Wind, Bioelectricity (includes BECCS), Geothermal and hydropower, and Fossil Carbon Capture and Storage (CCS). It is listed simply as a label in an energy options chart — there is no detailed discussion, analysis, or narrative text about nuclear energy in the report.

In the Full Volume (186 pages), the third mention on page 171 is incidental — it appears in an author's affiliation: "Federal Ministry for the Environment, Nature Conservation and Nuclear Safety" (Germany), which is not about nuclear energy as a topic.

### Key finding
Nuclear energy is mentioned on only **1 page** in the ~85-page Longer Report, and it appears solely as a label in an energy mitigation options diagram, with no substantive discussion. [Source: IPCC AR6 SYR Longer Report, page 69; IPCC AR6 SYR Full Volume, pages 43, 119, 171]

input.command:
# Try the DOI direct link
curl -L -s -o /tmp/ipcc_report.pdf -w "%{http_code} %{size_download}" "https://doi.org/10.59327/IPCC/AR6-9789291691647" 2>&1

# Extract any PDF links from the DOI landing page
grep -oi 'https://[^"]*\.pdf' /tmp/ipcc_report.pdf | head -20

file /tmp/ipcc_full.pdf && python3 -c "
import subprocess
result = subprocess.run(['pdftotext', '/tmp/ipcc_full.pdf', '-', '-layout'], capture_output=True, text=True)
text = result.stdout
# Find all lines mentioning nuclear
lines = text.split('\n')
nuclear_lines = [(i, line) for i, line in enumerate(lines) if 'nuclear' in line.lower()]
print(f'Total lines: {len(lines)}')
print(f'Lines mentioning nuclear: {len(nuclear_lines)}')
for idx, line in nuclear_lines:
    print(f'Line {idx}: {line[:200]}')
"

python3 -c "
import subprocess
result = subprocess.run(['pdftotext', '/tmp/ipcc_full.pdf', '-', '-layout'], capture_output=True, text=True)
text = result.stdout
lines = text.split('\n')

# Get context around nuclear mentions
for i, line in enumerate(lines):
    if 'nuclear' in line.lower():
        start = max(0, i-5)
        end = min(len(lines), i+5)
        print(f'=== Context around line {i} ===')
        for j in range(start, end):
            marker = '>>>' if j == i else '   '
            print(f'{marker} {j}: {lines[j][:250]}')
        print()
"

python3 -c "
import subprocess
# Get page numbers for nuclear mentions
result = subprocess.run(['pdftotext', '/tmp/ipcc_full.pdf', '-', '-layout', '-f', '1', '-l', '200'], capture_output=True, text=True)

# Also get total page count
result2 = subprocess.run(['pdfinfo', '/tmp/ipcc_full.pdf'], capture_output=True, text=True)
print(result2.stdout)

# Find what pages contain nuclear
import re
# Use pdftotext with page markers
result3 = subprocess.run(['pdftotext', '/tmp/ipcc_full.pdf', '/tmp/ipcc_full.txt', '-layout'], capture_output=True, text=True)
print('Done extracting')
"

python3 -c "
import subprocess

# Extract each page separately and check for 'nuclear'
nuclear_pages = []
for page in range(1, 187):
    result = subprocess.run(['pdftotext', '/tmp/ipcc_full.pdf', '-', '-f', str(page), '-l', str(page)], capture_output=True, text=True)
    if 'nuclear' in result.stdout.lower():
        nuclear_pages.append(page)

print(f'Total pages: 186')
print(f'Pages mentioning nuclear: {len(nuclear_pages)}')
print(f'Page numbers: {nuclear_pages}')
"

# Check if there's an 85-page version (perhaps the Longer Report or SPM)
# Let's check the Longer Report separately
curl -L -s -o /tmp/ipcc_longer.pdf -w "%{http_code} %{size_download}" "https://www.ipcc.ch/report/ar6/syr/downloads/report/IPCC_AR6_SYR_LongerReport.pdf" 2>&1

python3 -c "
import subprocess

# Get page count for Longer Report
result = subprocess.run(['pdfinfo', '/tmp/ipcc_longer.pdf'], capture_output=True, text=True)
for line in result.stdout.split('\n'):
    if 'Pages' in line:
        print(f'Longer Report: {line}')

# Check for nuclear
nuclear_pages = []
result2 = subprocess.run(['pdfinfo', '/tmp/ipcc_longer.pdf'], capture_output=True, text=True)
pages_match = [l for l in result2.stdout.split('\n') if 'Pages' in l]
total_pages = int(pages_match[0].split(':')[1].strip())

for page in range(1, total_pages + 1):
    result = subprocess.run(['pdftotext', '/tmp/ipcc_longer.pdf', '-', '-f', str(page), '-l', str(page)], capture_output=True, text=True)
    if 'nuclear' in result.stdout.lower():
        nuclear_pages.append(page)

print(f'Total pages: {total_pages}')
print(f'Pages mentioning nuclear: {len(nuclear_pages)}')
print(f'Page numbers: {nuclear_pages}')
"

python3 -c "
import subprocess

result = subprocess.run(['pdfinfo', '/tmp/ipcc_spm.pdf'], capture_output=True, text=True)
for line in result.stdout.split('\n'):
    if 'Pages' in line:
        print(f'SPM: {line}')

nuclear_pages = []
pages_match = [l for l in result.stdout.split('\n') if 'Pages' in l]
total_pages = int(pages_match[0].split(':')[1].strip())

for page in range(1, total_pages + 1):
    r = subprocess.run(['pdftotext', '/tmp/ipcc_spm.pdf', '-', '-f', str(page), '-l', str(page)], capture_output=True, text=True)
    if 'nuclear' in r.stdout.lower():
        nuclear_pages.append(page)

print(f'Total pages: {total_pages}')
print(f'Pages mentioning nuclear: {len(nuclear_pages)}')
print(f'Page numbers: {nuclear_pages}')
"

# Get context of nuclear mentions in each document
python3 -c "
import subprocess

for name, fpath in [('SPM (42 pages)', '/tmp/ipcc_spm.pdf'), ('Longer Report (81 pages)', '/tmp/ipcc_longer.pdf'), ('Full Volume (186 pages)', '/tmp/ipcc_full.pdf')]:
    result = subprocess.run(['pdftotext', fpath, '-', '-layout'], capture_output=True, text=True)
    lines = result.stdout.split('\n')
    print(f'=== {name} ===')
    for i, line in enumerate(lines):
        if 'nuclear' in line.lower():
            start = max(0, i-8)
            end = min(len(lines), i+3)
            for j in range(start, end):
                marker = '>>>' if j == i else '   '
                print(f'{marker} {lines[j][:200]}')
            print()
    print()
"

| Version | Pages | Pages mentioning "nuclear" | Nuclear page numbers |

### Answer for the "85 pages version"
The **Longer Report (Sections)** at **81 pages** is the closest match to the "85 pages version" you refer to. In this version, nuclear energy is mentioned on **1 page** (page 69).

### Context of the mentions
In all versions, "Nuclear" appears in the same context: as one item in a **figure/diagram listing energy supply mitigation options**, alongside Solar, Wind, Bioelectricity (includes BECCS), Geothermal and hydropower, and Fossil Carbon Capture and Storage (CCS). It is listed simply as a label in an energy options chart — there is no detailed discussion, analysis, or narrative text about nuclear energy in the report.

In the Full Volume (186 pages), the third mention on page 171 is incidental — it appears in an author's affiliation: "Federal Ministry for the Environment, Nature Conservation and Nuclear Safety" (Germany), which is not about nuclear energy as a topic.
